uses: Tragacantha cobrensis, commonly known as the 'Squirrel Tail' grass, has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its medicinal uses include treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery, due to its anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Tragacantha cobrensis, also known as French marigold, is a flowering plant belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce fever, and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Tragacantha cobrensis include carotenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as topical creams and ointments for skin care.
